title: Mouse Traps, 6 Pack Heavy Duty Reusable Snap Traps that Kill Instantly, High Sensitive Mice Catcher, Safe and Effective Rodent Trap for Indoors Outdoors
features: ⚠️【Be Patient Before Catch Them】Most mice and rats are inherently suspicious of new objects in their surroundings. If you don’t catch any one of them, don’t worry. Some rodents get caught easily on the first night. But many of them are very alert to new objects. Teach them to trust traps by using baits that they enjoy. It's time to catch them as long as they're at ease with the new objects.

👍【0 Escape Rate but Humane】These mouse rat traps are also upgraded with 1s trigger and thick powerful spring. That means that as long as the mouse or rat touches the pedal, it will get caught within 1s. There’s no way to escape from. However, these snap traps are also no bleeding teeth design, which means that they will get caught with less pain. So if you don’t want them to get killed, just simply release them by pressing the bar to the end.

👍【Easy to Set Up & Clean】These Mice Traps are designed with a detachable bait cup and made from premium ABS & Steel material. Just pull out the bait cup and fill it with bait, and then press the bar to the end. Done, the trap is all set. You can clean it simply by water when they get dirty.
👍【What You Get】6x Mouse Traps. 1X User Guide. Lifetime 100% worries-free after purchase service. If you have any questions concerning the rat traps, we’re always here to help.
description: 6 Common Mouse Trap Mistakes: 🟠 1.Patience Most mice and rats are inherently suspicious of new objects in their surroundings. Teach them to trust traps by using baits that they enjoy. It's time to catch them as long as they're at ease with the new objects. 🟠 2. Don’t Touch Traps or Baits with Bare Hands Human Scents will frighten them away. 🟠 3. Using the wrong type of Bait - No take-away Baits Don’t use cheese baits. Forget about the old cartoon depiction of mice eating cheese. Some mice enjoy cheese, while the majority do not. Use carbohydrate-rich foods, such as chocolate, or grain and seed-based foods, such as peanut butter and Nutella. When baiting for mice in the winter, using nest-building materials such as cotton balls and dental floss may be advantageous. 🟠 4. Don’t Use too much bait Make sure you only use a small amount of bait to tempt them into triggering the trap, this will prevent them from snatching bait without standing on the trap. 🟠 5. Setting enough traps Mice and rats are prolific breeders, so keep that in mind! If you've seen one mouse, there's a good chance there’s more. You'll have a greater chance of catching as many as possible if you place many traps every 2-3 feet. 🟠 6.Wrong Placement Keep the traps close to the wall and away from open areas. Mice scamper about the perimeters of rooms and the dark corners of your home, close to the walls because they have a natural phobia of open places. ❤️100% SATISFACTION GUARANTEE-We’re dedicated to offering great products with premium quality under strict and highest standards. Contact us if any problem, we provide timely response and perfect solution
